level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: The Operator as a Service (OaaS) vision is a novel paradigm where skilled remote operators are empowered with advanced digital technologies based on digital twins to remotely oversee and control multiple autonomous and flexible smart manufacturing lines. This paper proposes the application of Asset Administration Shell (AAS) to support a scalable deployment of the OaaS vision. Remote operators and flexible manufacturing lines are connected through an AAS dataspace, which provides connectivity and security and traceability functionalities. Furthermore, both operators and manufacturing lines can control with whom they collaborate. Once remote operators and manufacturing lines are connected, every time an unexpected situation occurs, the remote operator receives a notification to connect to the digital twin of the line to evaluate the situation and to solve it. This approach has been implemented based on Eclipse Basyx and AAS Dataspace for Everybody, extending an existing autonomous assembly line testbed and successfully validating it during the 2025 edition of the Hannover Messe. © 2025 IEEE.

title: IoT and 5G Integration: Transforming Real-Time Applications in Smart Industries
authors: Nandy M.; Dubey A.
source_title: 2025 International Conference on Automation and Computation, AUTOCOM 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - IoT and 5G Integration Transforming Real-Time Applications in Smart Industries.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: Modern M2M communication engaged with IoT and 5G technology is reinventing real time applications in smart industries through provision of efficient connectivity, low latency and large number of devices connection. This synchronizes makes it possible to transform industries, and hence lay down the groundwork for Industry 4.0 and more. The IM connectivity along with increased reliability and velocity made available by 5G technology improve IoT networks; key service use cases include predictive maintenance, robotic control, and real-time information analytics. Through network slicing, the key application areas can be prioritized to get the right of way and optimal resource consumptions in the 5G applications. In addition, the compatibility of IoT systems with high 5G networks resolves the difficulties that arise from various industrial settings, including the coordination of large amounts of data produced by the IoT devices. It also enhances energy use efficiency, decreases operational expenses and fosters sustainability, due to the use of improved resource optimisation. It notes that manufacturing logistics and healthcare key industrial areas have experienced changes through areas such as remote monitoring, digital twins, and augmented reality applications. This paper examines the technological convergence between IoT and 5G, showcases innovative industrial use cases, and advances opportunities and threats of the integration to establish intelligent and efficient sectors in the context of the internet of things. © 2025 IEEE.

title: DTInsight: A Tool for Explicit, Interactive, and Continuous Digital Twin Reporting
authors: Fiter K.; Malassigné-Onfroy L.; Oakes B.
source_title: Proceedings - 2025 ACM/IEEE 28th International Conference on Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems Companion, MODELS-C 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - DTInsight A Tool for Explicit, Interactive, and Continuous Digital Twin Reporting.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: With Digital Twin (DT) construction and evolution occurring over time, stakeholders require tools to understand the current characteristics and conceptual architecture of the system at any time. We introduce DTInsight, a systematic and automated tool and methodology for producing continuous reporting for DTs. DTInsight offers three key features: (a) an interactive conceptual architecture visualization of DTs; (b) generation of summaries of DT characteristics based on ontological data; and (c) integration of these outputs into a reporting page within a continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline. Given a modeled description of the DT aligning to our DT Description Framework (DTDF), DTInsight enables up-to-date and detailed reports for enhanced stakeholder understanding. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Interconnecting Urban Networks: A Novel Approach to Digital Twins Through GlassBox Adaptation
authors: Costagliola A.R.; Montanari M.; Bellavista P.
source_title: International Conference on Smart Cities and Green ICT Systems, SMARTGREENS - Proceedings
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Interconnecting Urban Networks A Novel Approach to Digital Twins Through GlassBox Adaptation.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is DT-related but clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks a validation/update signal.
abstract: Smart cities are transforming urban living by leveraging technology and data to optimize urban services, to improve resource efficiency, and to promote sustainability. Urban Digital Twins (UDTs) promise to play a central enabling role in this transformation. However, state-of-the-art digital twin models still have to address significant challenging issues, particularly in terms of interoperability and integration with complex and multilayered (often legacy) urban systems. Some emerging approaches rely on specific standards and ontologies, creating”information silos” peculiar to each digital twin solution. After discussing the related and still open technical challenges, this paper proposes a novel extension of the GlassBox urban simulation model to conceptualize a city as a multilayered network, where nodes can be entities at different levels of granularity, such as a single building, a urban energy network, or even an aggregated urban area. Our proposed solution aims to ensure interoperability between these layers by enabling seamless real-time data exchange and optimized resource management. Furthermore, integration between layers such as energy, transportation, and water is essential to ensure data synchronization and provide the basis for more advanced smart city services, e.g., energy consumption/production prediction. To practically exemplify the advantages of the proposed approach, our innovative model is also illustrated when supporting a case study that focuses on urban transportation systems. Copyright © 2025 by SCITEPRESS - Science and Technology Publications, Lda.

title: A Literature Review of H-BIM Strategy for Construction Maintenance
authors: Morganti C.; Bragadin M.A.
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - A Literature Review of H-BIM Strategy for Construction Maintenance.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks transferable validation/update signal.
abstract: Building maintenance planning strategy has always been of capital importance to improve building asset life cycle, and Building Information modelling can improve the efficiency of maintenance processes. The research work under this paper performed a literature review addressing the H-BIM strategy for building maintenance management and planning. Heritage Building Information Modeling H-BIM, is the application of the BIM strategy to the existing building stock, with the aim of creating a method and tools for building information acquisition and management. Actual implementation of H-BIM based strategy for building maintenance management aims at planning and controlling technical maintenance operations, also addressing environmental sustainability. Sustainable building maintenance mainly refers to materials and product re-use. Therefore, in case of existing buildings without cultural value, building maintenance sustainability refers to the Design for Deconstruction problem and H-BIM can be used to improve End of Life sustainability. H-BIM contains all pieces of information available to minimize Construction and Demolition Waste while deconstructing and reusing the building materials in a circular economy perspective. Digital Twin is the virtual replica of a built asset, that can support analysis, control and simulation functions with the objective of creating a Decision Support System for maintenance management. The use of sensor systems distributed within the building will become essential tools in the maintenance process. The owner will always be updated about building functions efficiency, and related maintenance requirements and operations. Even the model of use of the building can be designed and controlled, addressing specific living experiences of the users, the Building-Enabled lifestyle management. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2025.

title: Towards a digitally enabled intelligent coal mine integrated energy system: Evolution, conceptualization, and implementation
authors: Zeng B.; Yang X.; Hu P.; Wang Y.; Dong H.; Gong D.; Ye X.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Towards a digitally enabled intelligent coal mine integrated energy system Evolution, conceptualization, and implementation.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: The consensus on energy-saving and low-carbon goals led to greater requirements for the sustainable development of coal mines (CMs) worldwide. Against the above backdrop, the concept of Coal Mine Integrated Energy System (CMIES), a promising solution for coping with the smartization and decarbonization issues in the future development of CMs, has been proposed. However, the complexities of CM structure and operations, the shortage of automation and intelligent control techniques, and the stringent operation criteria, lead to high energy consumption, inefficient resource utilization, and unsatisfactory renewable energy accommodation in the mining system. Here, research on the emerging digital technologies and their potential applications is reviewed to provide reference for enhancing energy efficiency and mitigating emissions in future CM energy systems. First, the evolution of mining energy production and consumption paradigms is summarized from the perspective of technological advancements. Subsequently, the need to develop digitally enabled CMIES through a combined economic-security-emission analysis is discussed. To overcome the obstacles hindering the digital transformation of CM energy systems, a hierarchical implementation framework for digitally enabled CMIES involving the value, physical, and information layers is proposed. Furthermore, an intelligent information system incorporating device sensing and intelligence technology, new information network, big data, and digital twin is designed, and specific application scenarios are presented. Finally, the challenges and opportunities for future development of digitally enabled CMIES are discussed based on the Strengths-Weaknesses-Opportunities-Threats (SWOT) analysis. © 2024 Elsevier Ltd

title: Urban flooding digital twin system framework
authors: Ge C.; Qin S.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Urban flooding digital twin system framework.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is DT-related but clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks a validation/update signal.
abstract: Digital twin-based resilience management systems are essential for improving urban flooding resilience, enabling lifecycle-oriented emergency management as a continuous process rather than discrete phases. However, to the best of our knowledge, currently, there lacks a system framework for supporting the development of an urban flooding digital twin (UFDT) platform capable of managing urban flooding events across the entire management lifecycle. This UFDT framework is required to (1) accommodate UFDT model-generation methods/tools for preparing what-if scenarios, (2) support readiness simulations and evaluations, (3) coordinate various collaborative prevention and intervention services from different stakeholders in response to any flooding emergence, and (4) monitor and forecast the flooding risks in the recovery phase. To fill this gap, in this paper, a new UFDT system framework is developed based on a user-centred product design process with the consideration of the above requirements. It has two key components: a UFDT conceptual model, and a generative methodology for its rapid construction/updating/adaptation to varying levels of detail. A framework prototype has been developed for testing the conceptual model at city, regional and street levels and exemplar generative methods/tools to assess the framework’s ability and potential to provide scalable, adaptable, and stakeholder-focused solutions to urban flooding resilience management[Q1]. © 2025 The Author(s). Published by Informa UK Limited, trading as Taylor & Francis Group.

title: Applicability of MongoDB for Smart City Digital Twin Implementation
authors: Ripley C.; Qian M.; Wang Y.; Yu W.
source_title: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 23rd International Conference on Software Engineering Research, Management and Applications, SERA 2025 - Proceedings
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Applicability of MongoDB for Smart City Digital Twin Implementation.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is DT-related but clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks a validation/update signal.
abstract: The fast development of smart cities includes edging technologies such as Digital Twin (DT) that plays a critical role in developing and managing smart cities by providing dynamic, virtual representations of physical assets, systems, and processes. These digital replicas are continuously updated with real-time data collected from Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as sensors, smart devices, connected vehicles, and monitoring systems, to enable city planners, policymakers, and operators to monitor, analyze, and optimize urban environments with unprecedented precision. To gain meaningful insights from collected data, it is necessary to have robust database systems capable of efficiently storing, organizing, and retrieving large volumes of information. Databases serve as the backbone of smart city applications, ensuring that IoT-generated data can be accessed quickly and reliably to support analytics, predictive modeling, and real-time operations. This paper examines MongoDB, a widely used nonrelational database technology, with the objective of exploring various database architectures and features. The analysis focuses on how these architectural choices influence the performance of smart city applications, providing insights into the factors that should be considered when selecting a database solution for IoTdriven environments.  © 2025 IEEE.

title: Integrating Digital Twins and Extended Reality for Smart PSS Design: A Case Study on Smart Electric Tour Bus Development
authors: Yan Z.; Larsson T.; Haoqiang W.; Chunjun H.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Integrating Digital Twins and Extended Reality for Smart PSS Design A Case Study on Smart Electric Tour Bus Development.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: The increasing digitization of manufacturing is transforming product development towards intelligent, service-oriented systems. This study explores the application of Digital Twins (DT) technology in smart Product Service System (sPSS) design, proposing a framework of Super-System Digital Twin (SSDT) and eXtended Reality (XR) for supporting design. The SSDT integrates service offerings, user scenarios, and product features throughout the design process. Case is applied to the conceptual design of a Smart Electric Tourist Bus in collaboration with a global manufacturer, the framework utilizes eXtended Reality (XR) environment to enhance decision-making and prototyping. This research validates SSDT's role in fostering participatory design and collaborative innovation, while addressing its limitations and proposing future improvements.  © 2025 The Authors.

title: Information-centric production information management (IcPIM) framework for the consistent use of production planning-related data in the automotive industry
authors: Vogt A.; Stark R.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Information-centric production information management (IcPIM) framework for the consistent use of production planning-related data in the automotive industry.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks transferable validation/update signal.
abstract: Production planning is the linking function between product development and production and therefore an important stakeholder of PLM systems in the automobile manufacturing industry, aiming to manage data throughout various lifecycle stages. Yet, state of the art PLM data structures present challenges because of their lack of flexibility and automation concerning needs-orientated data structures addressing the data domains of products, processes and resources. Another crucial challenge is the transferability of data throughout different applications in a diverse IT-landscape in high volume, customization and complexity industries. This work presents a concept for the management of production planning-related data structures for consistent use from product development and production planning up to production IT and Digital Twin applications. It is developed based on a large-scale case study in an automobile OEM group and validated in concept trial studies as well as an ongoing organizational change project towards data governance in the corporate context. © 2025 The Author(s). Published by Informa UK Limited, trading as Taylor & Francis Group.

title: Exploring Automotive Quality Correlations through Explainable Machine Learning What-If Simulation
authors: Alexandre O., Jr.; Calvo-Rolle J.L.; Pires R.; Leitao P.
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Exploring Automotive Quality Correlations through Explainable Machine Learning What-If Simulation.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks transferable validation/update signal.
abstract: High-dimensional variability in manufacturing processes presents significant challenges for quality control, demanding predictive strategies capable of capturing complex parameter dependencies. Machine learning (ML) offers robust mechanisms for this purpose, but reliance on black-box models often limits interpretability and hinders producing stakeholders' identification of meaningful correlations for model optimization. This paper introduces an interactive what-if simulation platform designed to explore structural quality correlations in automotive assembly through explainable ML techniques, enhancing transparency and enabling uncertainty quantification. The platform is based on a modular Digital Twin (DT) architecture aligned with the ISO 23247 standard, guiding expert and non-expert users through correlation-driven feature selection, regression modelling and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) based post-hoc explanations. A case study using real inspection data from a vehicle assembly line demonstrates the tool's capacity to support variable relevance assessment, dimensionality reduction, and model interpretability. Furthermore, an uncertainty-aware SHAP analysis enhances confidence in the model's prediction stability, reinforcing the platform's suitability for quality-driven decision support and integration into future DT ecosystems. © 2025 IEEE.

title: A Hybrid Digital Twin Framework for Stability Analysis in Dynamic Systems
authors: Rocha D.; Pinto R.; Goncalves G.
source_title: IEEE International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Factory Automation, ETFA
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - A Hybrid Digital Twin Framework for Stability Analysis in Dynamic Systems.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Effectively integrating knowledge-based data with data-driven techniques in hybrid digital twins (HDTs) is essential for advancing monitoring capabilities in Industry 4.0. This integration involves leveraging domain knowledge (physics) to guide model structure, inform training data, or reveal new parameters and behaviours, while data is used to refine models, improve accuracy, estimate unknown parameters, and capture phenomena not considered in theoretical approaches. To address this challenge, we propose a novel HDT framework, inspired by Landau-Ginzburg Theory (LGT) of phase transitions, that enables the digital twin to be computationally lightweight and physically interpretable. In this study, machine learning-based production data simulation was applied to a real SIF-400 smart assembly line training system, serving as a conceptual case study. This allows modelling of the operating conditions in such systems. Three operating phases - Optimal, Stressed, and Failed - are characterised by a stability function. Analysis of the corresponding stability profiles validates the framework's ability to classify system states, highlighting its potential for holistic stability assessment and early detection of critical transitions in system dynamic processes. Bi-directional communication enables automatic system stoppage upon failure detection. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Hybrid Physics-LSTM Framework for Wind Power Prediction and Control in Virtual Microgrid Simulations
authors: López Flórez S.; Hernández González G.; Prieto J.; de la Prieta F.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Hybrid Physics-LSTM Framework for Wind Power Prediction and Control in Virtual Microgrid Simulations.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Three-dimensional physical systems play a pivotal role in the development of cyber-physical infrastructures, particularly in the implementation of digital twins that enable the evaluation of hypothetical and adverse scenarios through high-fidelity simulation. This work presents a real-time 3D monitoring and feedback system designed for a custom transverse-axis wind turbine, integrating physical modeling principles with simulation engines developed initially for game environments. This hybrid architecture facilitates the virtual prototyping, testing, and validation of wind energy systems under near-operational conditions. The proposed framework combines two key components: 1) a physics-based model grounded in the mechanical and electromagnetic dynamics of wind turbine operation, and 2) a data-driven architecture composed of multiple layers. The physical layer interfaces directly with the sensors and actuators of the turbine, ensuring real-time synchronization between the physical and virtual systems. Data acquisition and communication are managed through the MQTT protocol, enabling low-latency streaming and robust interoperability. A long short-term memory neural network is integrated into the architecture to enhance predictive capabilities and trained to forecast wind energy production. An intelligent battery management system subsequently utilizes the output of the model to optimize charging strategies. © 2013 IEEE.

title: Data-Driven Technologies for Energy Optimization in Smart Buildings: A Scoping Review
authors: Billanes J.D.; Ma Z.G.; Jørgensen B.N.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Data-Driven Technologies for Energy Optimization in Smart Buildings A Scoping Review.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks transferable validation/update signal.
abstract: Data-driven technologies in smart buildings offer significant opportunities to enhance energy efficiency, sustainability, and occupant comfort. However, the existing literature often lacks a holistic examination of the technological advancements, adoption barriers, and business models necessary to realize these benefits. To address this gap, this scoping review synthesizes current research on these technologies, identifies factors influencing their adoption, and examines supporting business models. Inspired by the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) guidelines, a structured search of the literature across four major databases yielded 112 relevant studies. The key technologies identified included big data analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, the Internet of Things, Wireless Sensor Networks, Edge and Cloud Computing, Blockchain, Digital Twins, and Geographic Information Systems. Energy optimization is further achieved through integrating renewable energy resources and advanced energy management systems, such as Home Energy Management Systems and Building Energy Management Systems. Factors influencing adoption are categorized into social influences, individual perceptions, cost considerations, security and privacy concerns, and data quality issues. The analysis of business models emphasizes the need to align technological innovations with market needs, focusing on value propositions like cost savings and efficiency improvements. Despite the benefits, challenges such as high initial costs, technical complexities, security risks, and user acceptance hinder their widespread adoption. This review highlights the importance of addressing these challenges through the development of cost-effective, interoperable, secure, and user-centric solutions, offering a roadmap for future research and industry applications. © 2025 by the authors.

title: Dynamic Adaptive Charging Network Planning Under Deep Uncertainties
authors: Saqib E.; Gidófalvi G.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2024 - Dynamic Adaptive Charging Network Planning Under Deep Uncertainties.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: Charging infrastructure is the backbone of electromobility. Due to new charging behaviors and power distribution and charging space constraints, the energy demand and supply patterns of electromobility and the locations of current refueling stations are misaligned. Infrastructure developers (charging point operators, fleet operators, grid operators, vehicle manufacturers, and real-estate developers) need new methodologies and tools that help reduce the cost and risk of investments. To this extent we propose a transport-energy-demand-centric, dynamic adaptive planning approach and a data-driven Spatial Decision Support System (SDSS). In the SDSS, with the help of a realistic digital twin of an electrified road transport system, infrastructure developers can quickly and accurately estimate key performance measures (e.g., charging demand, Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) enablement) of a candidate charging location or a network of locations under user-specified transport electrification scenarios and constraints and interactively and continuously calibrate and/or expand their network plans as facts about the deep uncertainties about the supply side of transport electrification (i.e., access to grid capacity and real-estate and presence of competition) are gradually discovered/observed. This paper describes the components and the planning support of the SDSS and how these can be used in competitive and collaborative settings. Qualitative user evaluations of the SDSS with 33 stakeholder organizations in commercial discussions and pilots have shown that both transport-energy-demand-centric and dynamic adaptive planning of charging infrastructure planning are useful. © 2024 by the authors.

title: Collision-Free Trajectory Planning in Cluttered Environments for Efficient Bin Picking
authors: Xu X.; Bashir A.; Bhagiya J.; Muller R.
source_title: IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Collision-Free Trajectory Planning in Cluttered Environments for Efficient Bin Picking.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: In cluttered, reconfigurable environments with varied objects, materials, and sizes - where obstacles can be added, removed, or moved - planning robot arm actions between grasps is a key challenge for traditional bin picking. We propose a trajectory planner that quickly determines how to grasp and precisely assemble customized products in reconfigurable environment. Inspired by voting and global registration methods, our strategy reduces false positives and enhances object identification accuracy, even with vastly different point cloud scales. We present an image processing technique combining best-fit and iterative closest point methods to improve system robustness, adaptability, and stability for objects of varying shapes, sizes, and materials, achieving millimeter-level 3D localization with millions of scene points. An enhanced tree method manages uneven 3D space distribution, enabling fast collision-free planning. A space configuration algorithm minimizes computational load, supporting complex tasks like liquid handling. After digital twin verification of collision-free paths, the robotic arm executes assembly tasks. This approach is validated through Lego assembly and other industrial use cases.  © 2025 IEEE.

title: PRISM-IWMS: A Privacy-Preserving, Resource-Aware, Incentive-Aligned Digital-Twin Framework for Waste Management System
authors: Sarao P.K.
source_title: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Computing, Communication and Automation, ICCCA 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - PRISM-IWMS A Privacy-Preserving, Resource-Aware, Incentive-Aligned Digital-Twin Framework for Waste Management System.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: The population explosion has led to more solid waste production. It is not limited to urban populations and city growth, but the rural areas are also contributing to the greater extent. There is no spec of doubt that current urban management systems require better waste disposal solutions because traditional labor-based waste collection methods have proven insufficient despite being into extensive trend for longer period. On the other hand, rural population is also meager to find solutions to this massive issue. With the evolution of technology and latest innovations, the tech-savvy are coming up modern and techie solutions to almost all the problems. So, Smart waste management systems result from IWMS integration of IoT technology with artificial intelligence as well as machine learning capabilities. GIS operates together with modern technologies which combine cloud computing and data analytics and GIS platforms. The paper thoroughly investigates IWMS systems through an in-depth study of their vital technologies while examining system design and their benefits, challenges and emerging research directions. The study introduces PRISM-IWMS, a privacy-preserving, resource-aware, incentive-aligned framework that unifies federated multimodal learning, energy-aware sensing, and a causal digital twin for intelligent waste management along with metrics spanning operations. © 2025 IEEE.

title: IoT-Based Digital Twin Dashboard for Mushroom Cultivation Monitoring Using a Cloud-Based Data Visualization Platform
authors: Yanti N.N.A.K.; Alam E.N.; Suakanto S.; Lathifah S.N.
source_title: 2025 International Conference on Information and Communication Technology, ICoICT 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - IoT-Based Digital Twin Dashboard for Mushroom Cultivation Monitoring Using a Cloud-Based Data Visualization Platform.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Oyster mushroom cultivation is an agricultural sector that is sensitive to changes in temperature and humidity. However, at the MSME level, environmental monitoring is still mostly done manually with intuition, making it prone to errors and decreasing its productivity. This research aims to develop an IoT-based monitoring system visualized through a twin digital dashboard using the Looker Studio cloud-based platform. The system is built with DHT11 and Raspberry Pi sensors that send temperature and humidity data to a cloud database and display it in a dashboard that can be accessed remotely with an update interval every 10 minutes. Usability evaluation was conducted using the PSSUQ instrument which includes three dimensions, namely System Usefulness, Information Quality, and Interface Quality. The evaluation results showed an excellent score with an overall average of 1.63, reflecting ease of use, information quality, and system suitability to the needs of non-technical users. Unlike previous studies that relied on closed or paid platforms, this system offers an open, free, and easily customizable solution. The main contribution of this research is in addressing the challenges of limited technology and digital literacy of MSMEs by providing an inclusive, efficient, and contextual visualization approach for the small-scale agriculture sector. © 2025 IEEE.

title: UTwinVM: Reliable hints on the effects of hypervisor updates on VMs in the Cloud
authors: Mvondo D.; Xing T.; Barbalace A.
source_title: Middleware 2024 - Proceedings of the 25th ACM International Middleware Conference
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2024 - UTwinVM Reliable hints on the effects of hypervisor updates on VMs in the Cloud.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: We investigate the problem of getting hints on the effects of virtualization system (aka hypervisor) updates impact on virtual machines (VMs). System administrators can be reluctant to apply updates due to vague hints regarding the updates’ impact on running applications. The problem is challenging since VMs are black boxes by design, reducing the scope of the data that can be retrieved and analyzed. Additionally, cloning VMs is only sometimes possible for obvious legal and privacy concerns. In this paper, we present UTwinVM for Updated Twin VM, a mechanism to obtain valuable hints concerning the impact of updates on applications running in VMs. UTwinVM key idea is to generate a digital twin of running VMs that mimics the original VMs workloads behavior if they were running on with the updated virtualization stack. To achieve that, UTwinVM records several metrics regarding running VMs and the virtualization system on the initial server and on another server where the intended updated system runs. Then, it leverages a non-linear negative squared solver to determine how the initial system differs from the updated one. Based on that, it runs through specific scripts, workloads in VMs will match workloads running in the production VMs as if they were running on the updated system. Consequently, system administrators can observe these recreated workloads to obtain hints on the potential performance impact on production VMs. UTwinVM is non-intrusive for VMs and does not require modifications to the virtualization system. We implement an open-source prototype of UTwinVM atop the Linux/KVM virtualization system (u6.6.4). Our evaluation shows that UTwinVM incurs no overhead on running VMs and less than 11% performance impact on host services while providing accurate hints for several update scenarios - up to 99%. © 2024 Copyright held by the owner/author(s).

title: Research on Intelligent Recommendation Methods for Manual Inspection of Electrical Systems in Complex Products
authors: Yu Y.; Zhao S.; Gao H.; Chen Y.; Xiang K.; Wang Z.
source_title: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering, ICEACE 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Research on Intelligent Recommendation Methods for Manual Inspection of Electrical Systems in Complex Products.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: The electrical network serves as an organic interconnection carrier for various systems in complex products, and its reliability is a prerequisite for product performance. However, during the product development process, technical conditions are always unstable. Existing automatic testing equipment faces high costs for simultaneous software and hardware updates, causing difficulties in wide applications. Thus, in these situations, many products still require manual operations for actual testing tasks. While manual testing offers a certain degree of flexibility, it also faces significant limitations, especially for large-scale, complex electrical networks where the testing process can be time-consuming. To address these issues, an intelligent testing method based on a connectivity sequence optimization model is proposed in this paper, which is integrated with the digital twin technology. This method is employed to the testing process of electrical networks, and provides personalized testing recommendations to operators, thereby further enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of the testing. The proposed method is not only suitable for products with currently unstable technical conditions but also has broad application prospects, making it applicable to other fields that require high-precision and high-efficiency testing. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Simulation of Multivariable Monitoring and Operational Fault Prediction Using a Digital Twin for ROVs in Subsea Installation Operations
authors: Barreto P.S.; Pinto M.F.
source_title: 27th Brazilian Symposium on Robotics and 26th Workshop on Robotics in Education, SBR-WRE 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Simulation of Multivariable Monitoring and Operational Fault Prediction Using a Digital Twin for ROVs in Subsea Installation Operations.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: The increasing complexity of offshore operations and the growing demand for efficiency, safety, and sustainability have driven the development of intelligent digital solutions. This study proposes a digital twin model for remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) applied to subsea installation activities, integrating multiple interdependent operational variables such as motor temperature, electric current, depth, and accumulated operating time. Using a synthetic Big Data set, the system applies multivariable monitoring, anomaly detection, and machine learning algorithms to perform real-time fault prediction. The results demonstrate the model's effectiveness in anticipating failures, generating contextual recommendations, and reducing unplanned downtime, thereby improving the reliability of subsea systems. Although validated with synthetic data, the methodology is designed for straightforward adaptation to real-world sensor data and can be extended to other offshore assets such as subsea trees, launch towers, offshore cranes, and subsea production systems. This approach supports ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) objectives by promoting safer, more efficient, and sustainable offshore operations.  © 2025 IEEE.

title: Precision aquaculture strategies under the integration of intelligent monitoring and digital twin
authors: Ding Y.; Lin M.
source_title: Proceedings of SPIE - The International Society for Optical Engineering
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Precision aquaculture strategies under the integration of intelligent monitoring and digital twin.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Recent advances in smart city ecosystems necessitate innovative approaches to optimize urban food production systems. This study investigates the synergistic implementation of IoT-based intelligent sensors and digital twin modeling for precision management in Marsupenaeus japonicus aquaculture operations. The methodology integrates multiparameter environmental sensing for real-time detection of dissolved oxygen, pH fluctuation patterns, and thermal gradients across culture ponds, coupled with dynamic digital replication of aquatic ecosystems through virtual modeling frameworks. A spatial-temporal data architecture was established to enable bidirectional interaction between physical operations and their digital counterparts, featuring real-time anomaly detection algorithms and predictive resource allocation models. Comparative analysis across varied culture configurations revealed 18-22% improvement in feed conversion efficiency through adaptive oxygenation control strategies, along with 30% reduction in disease incidence via neural network-based pathogen spread prediction. The system demonstrated particular efficacy in minimizing water resource consumption (15% decrease) while maintaining optimal biomass density thresholds. Visual analytics modules effectively translated complex environmental correlations into operational guidelines accessible to non-specialist operators, fostering technology adoption in conventional aquaculture facilities. The technological framework presents dual applicability for urban food systems and environmental governance, offering scalable solutions for nutrient discharge monitoring in coastal peri-urban zones and dynamic resource optimization in vertical aquaponics installations. Implementation pathways align with smart city development objectives through enhanced traceability in aquatic product supply chains and data-driven decision support for marine spatial planning. This converged physical-digital approach establishes foundation for transitioning traditional aquaculture toward circulatory production models that balance ecological integrity with protein production demands in urbanizing coastal regions.  © COPYRIGHT SPIE. Downloading of the abstract is permitted for personal use only.

title: AI-Powered HAZOP Methodologies Delivering Uniform, Reliable Outcomes with Improved Efficiency and Lower Expenditure
authors: Joshi A.; Shinde V.; Jagtap M.
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - AI-Powered HAZOP Methodologies Delivering Uniform, Reliable Outcomes with Improved Efficiency and Lower Expenditure.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: This study introduces an advanced AI-driven framework designed to overcome key challenges in conducting Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) integrated Hazard and Operability (HAZOP) assessments for petroleum and chemical processing facilities. Built on the proprietary Insights AI platform and expert-curated datasets from ELIXIR Consultants UK Ltd, the framework combines expert-led process mapping with automation to accelerate analysis while improving accuracy, consistency, and comprehensive risk evaluation. The approach begins with domain experts identifying critical process nodes, components, flows, and operational interfaces-such as equipment relationships, control systems, and high- and low-pressure connections-within process flow diagrams and engineering drawings. This expert validation ensures essential elements and their interdependencies are correctly captured before automation. Next, advanced computer vision techniques, including OpenCV, optical character recognition (OCR), and vision models, automate extraction of process elements from diagrams. These are cross-referenced against the expert framework, resulting in precise, machine-readable digital network models that capture equipment, instrumentation, and operational parameters. This digital twin supports robust downstream automation and integration with engineering workflows. A retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system then provides context-aware access to a rich knowledge base containing prior PHA-HAZOP studies, industry standards, technical documents, taxonomies, and historical references. Grounded in this domain knowledge, the Generative AI inference engine autonomously generates detailed deliverables: deviation scenarios, multiple potential causes, and consequence analyses focused on personnel safety, environmental impact, and asset damage. Importantly, the system evaluates existing safeguards, assesses residual risks, and recommends specific risk reduction actions to bring residual risks down to As Low As Reasonably Practicable (ALARP) levels. These insights are based on a prioritized risk ranking that integrates operational parameters, safeguard adequacy, and equipment relationships, effectively evolving traditional HAZOP into a comprehensive PHA-HAZOP process. An Agentic AI orchestration layer coordinates a network of specialized AI agents operating over a knowledge graph that models complex interrelations among equipment, control systems, safeguards, and risks. This multi-agent collaboration produces standardized, high-quality outputs that pre-populate PHA-HAZOP worksheets, enabling safety professionals to focus on complex systemic analysis and critical decision-making. By integrating vision-based diagram understanding, RAG knowledge retrieval, generative inference, and agentic orchestration, this scalable, transparent, and audit-compliant framework enhances automation and quality of PHA-HAZOP assessments in safety-critical engineering environments. Copyright © 2025, Society of Petroleum Engineers.

title: Hybrid Intelligent Decision Engine - Driven Multi-Agent System for Digital-Twin Inspection of Hydroturbine Blades
authors: Liu M.; Zhang K.; Pei F.
source_title: 2025 7th International Symposium on Robotics and Intelligent Manufacturing Technology, ISRIMT 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Hybrid Intelligent Decision Engine - Driven Multi-Agent System for Digital-Twin Inspection of Hydroturbine Blades.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: With the development of digital twin technology and the rapid emergence of artificial intelligence, intelligent inspection of complex curved components has become essential for enhancing the automation and intelligence level of hydropower equipment. This paper proposes a digital-twin-based inspection system for hydroturbine blades, centered on an AI-driven hybrid intelligent decision engine that integrates traditional geometric algorithms with the semantic reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs). Unlike conventional inspection methods that only output numerical deviations, the proposed system leverages NLP-based semantic understanding to perform defect classification, root-cause inference, and corrective action generation. A virtual commissioning model is constructed in Process Simulate to fully validate robot operations, 3D scanning workflows, and PLC logic prior to physical deployment. Moreover, through integration with the MES, the system establishes a real-time closed-loop quality control mechanism, enabling rapid feedback and adaptive production adjustment. Experimental results show that the proposed approach significantly outperforms traditional methods in detection accuracy, commissioning efficiency, and LLM-based defect semantic interpretation. This study provides new insights into the design of intelligent inspection units for complex curved components and effectively addresses key challenges in hydroturbine blade quality assurance. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Digital Twin Enabled Flight Control System Testing: Design, Development, and Implementation
authors: Ren C.; Xu J.; Chen C.; Zhu S.; Ma Y.; Guan X.
source_title: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Digital Twin Enabled Flight Control System Testing Design, Development, and Implementation.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Flight control system testing (FCST) is one of the most important process to check whether flight control surfaces can operate properly according to commands during aircraft assembly. Traditional testing method relies heavily on manual labor, leading to low efficiency and inconsistent quality. In this paper, we apply digital twin (DT) technology to the FCST process for the first time. We firstly design an architecture of DT-enabled FCST including four layers to support further development. Then, we present a triangular mesh alignment-based angle measurement (TMA-AM) algorithm to efficiently collect deflection angle data for DT-enabled FCST. Extensive experiments conducted on a aircraft wing subassembly platform show that the TMA-AM algorithm achieves an average angular measurement error of less than 0.1°, outperforming existing methods. Moreover, we develop a virtual experimental platform named DT-FCST aligned with a real aircraft wing subassembly platform. In addition, TMA-AM algorithm is integrated with the DT-FCST platform. By integrating real-time data from the cockpit, real-time physical-virtual interaction of aircraft control sticks and flight control surfaces are achieved, ensuring consistency between physical and virtual movements. The integration of DT technology with the TMA-AM algorithm enables real-time synchronization, monitoring, and unified data management, significantly enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of the FCST. Note to Practitioners - To address the inefficiencies and low monitoring quality associated with traditional manual testing methods in flight control system testing (FCST), we firstly introduce digital twin (DT) technology to this process. To support effective and accurate measurement during the FCST, we propose a vision-based method tailored to accurately measure deflection angles of flight control surfaces. This method replaces manual measurements with a non-contact approach, significantly improving measurement accuracy and efficiency. We provide a detailed description of the construction process of the DT-FCST platform including requirement analysis, DT model construction, and on-site experiments. This DT-based approach achieves real-time synchronization between virtual and physical testing processes, enhancing monitoring quality and overall testing effectiveness. Specifically, it can achieve a 90% reduction in the number of operators and shorten the single testing time to 16.7% of the traditional testing method.  © 2004-2012 IEEE.

title: Research on Adaptive Perception Real-Time Rendering Algorithm for Digital Twin Space Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Fang F.; Shen D.; Wang D.
source_title: Proceedings of 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization, IICSPI 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Research on Adaptive Perception Real-Time Rendering Algorithm for Digital Twin Space Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is DT-related but clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks a validation/update signal.
abstract: Real-time rendering of digital twin space is the core support for accurate interaction between virtual and real worlds. However, the contradiction between its dynamic scene characteristics and multi-objective optimization requirements is becoming increasingly prominent, and existing algorithms struggle to achieve dynamic adaptive optimization. To address this, a deep reinforcement learning-based adaptive rendering algorithm (DAVC-DRL) with dynamic attention-value coupling is proposed. This algorithm constructs a closed-loop architecture of 'perception-decision-execution-feedback,' accurately extracting multi-dimensional features such as scene complexity, user focus, and device resources through a dynamic attention mechanism; designs a discrete-continuous hybrid action space to achieve fine-tuning of rendering parameters; constructs a multi-objective value-coupled reward function to balance rendering quality, real-time performance, and resource utilization; and introduces an online adaptive update mechanism to quickly respond to dynamic changes in the digital twin space. Experimental results based on intelligent manufacturing workshops and smart city transportation scenarios show that the DAVC-DRL algorithm achieves an average rendering frame rate of 68.7 FPS, a 62.4% improvement over traditional adaptive algorithms and a 24.7% improvement over the conventional DQN algorithm; the average rendering latency is 14.5 ms, a 38.5% reduction compared to traditional algorithms; the peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) is 38.6 dB, and the structural similarity (SSIM) is 0.942. While maintaining high-quality rendering effects, the average GPU memory usage is only 8.7 GB, and the CPU utilization is 32.8%, demonstrating significantly optimized resource utilization efficiency. In scenarios with drastically changing dynamic targets, the frame rate fluctuation is only 7.2%, exhibiting strong adaptability. This algorithm provides a new and effective solution for real-time rendering of digital twin spaces and can be widely applied in fields such as intelligent manufacturing and smart cities. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Digital Twin Implementation in Small and Medium Size Enterprises: A Case Study
authors: Abolghasem S.; Carpitella S.; Mohan G.T.
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Digital Twin Implementation in Small and Medium Size Enterprises A Case Study.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: In this article, we delve into the integration of MTConnect-a standardized communication protocol for the exchange of manufacturing equipment data-as a unidirectional level digital twin technology in small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), aiming for digital transformation in the context of Industry 5.0. We examine how MTConnect serves as a linchpin for data standardization and data acquisition, providing a cost-effective and scalable solution that enables SMEs to leverage advanced analytics for predictive maintenance and operational control. The article presents a case study of IMOCOM Company’s adoption of MTConnect to enhance operational efficiency and reduce machine downtime, highlighting the development of a Decision Support System (DSS) using Microsoft Excel for data visualization and process monitoring. By focusing on the practical implementation and the ensuing benefits, the study emphasizes MTConnect’s transformative potential in smart manufacturing and the future avenues for its evolution to further empower SMEs in the digital age. © The Author(s), under exclusive license to Springer Nature Switzerland AG 2025.

title: Implementation & Application of coherent control systems: Digital Twin (high frequency open loop) with R2R (low frequency closed loop)
authors: Kotcher P.C.; Sarang K.; Allen M.; Dhandapani S.
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Implementation & Application of coherent control systems Digital Twin (high frequency open loop) with R2R (low frequency closed loop).pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: -A Digital Twin (Applied TwinTM) is a computational model that represents a physical asset such as a process chamber, evolving over time to reflect its structure, behavior, and context. This model treats the asset-twin system as a set of coupled dynamical systems that evolve over time, interacting through observed data and control inputs.[1] They are an evolution of modeling & simulation. The Digital Twin (DT) can be useful in integration, testing, monitoring, and maintenance. Eventually it can be utilized as an early and ad hoc fast learning tool for systems and processes. In semiconductor manufacturing, DTs have found their way in their early rudimentary form as control or predictive applications such as for production control applications like planning, scheduling, and dispatching and for equipment and process control applications like, Run-to-Run (Applied SmartFactoryTM Run-2-Run) (R2R), Virtual Metrology, and predictive maintenance. Through this paper we explore the idea of idea of engineering DT and R2R algorithms with intersecting system manipulated variables or inputs between the two systems. © 2025 IEEE.

title: A proof-of-concept study towards developing digital twins for operational excellence in large-scale water distribution networks
authors: Singh A.; Singh S.; Maheshwari A.
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - A proof-of-concept study towards developing digital twins for operational excellence in large-scale water distribution networks.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is DT-related but clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks a validation/update signal.
abstract: In the current face of water scarcity, water losses due to leakages in large-scale water distribution networks (WDN) and non-revenue water are challenging factors. In this direction, Digital Twins integrate concepts like IoT, ML (machine learning), and DL (deep learning) with a water pave path for smart urban water infrastructure. Herein, we propose a holistic digital twin systems framework and its application in leak detection, validated with field-data on Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IIT-J) campus WDN. A detailed methodology developing monitoring digital twins supported on the python platform and using open-source WDN simulators; EPANET and WNTR for hydraulic simulations and a Graph-convolution Neural Network-based leak detection model is elucidated. Results are analysed and demonstrated for the highest water consumption zone of the campus, with the model giving an accuracy of 90% for leakage detection. Further, a test scenario is described where the proposed framework shows water savings of up to 58% which would have been otherwise lost due to leaks in WDN. © 2025 Informa UK Limited, trading as Taylor & Francis Group.

title: Proposal to Improve Service Level in HDPE Barge Deliveries Using TSM-IoT, DDMRP, and Digital Twins
authors: Portugal G.D.H.; Tanamachi A.A.P.; Bardalez F.N.
source_title: Proceedings of the 2025 IEEE 32nd International Conference on Electronics, Electrical Engineering and Computing, INTERCON 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Proposal to Improve Service Level in HDPE Barge Deliveries Using TSM-IoT, DDMRP, and Digital Twins.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: This paper presents a proposal aimed at improving the service level in the delivery of floating barges made of high-density polyethylene (HDPE) at the Peruvian company SIGECO S.A.C., a project-based manufacturing company. The analysis identified the main obstacle as the poor performance of the OTIF (On Time In Full) indicator, caused by the lack of planning for distribution routes, insufficient logistics oversight, shortages of materials for final assembly, and delays in passing safety inspections. To address these challenges, the adoption of three technological tools aligned with Industry 4.0 principles is proposed: an IoT-enhanced Transportation Management System (TMS), the DDMRP methodology for materials planning, and the use of Digital Twins. The feasibility of the proposal was assessed through a quantitative analysis, the development of a technical design, and a review of its economic impact. The results showed improvements in the traceability of logistics operations, a reduction in assembly times, greater availability of input, and an increase in the level of customer service. In conclusion, the technical gap identified was mitigated, and the proposed solution demonstrates potential for replication in other industrial contexts with similar challenges in managing complex deliveries.  © 2025 IEEE.

title: Digital Twins in Surgery as a Real-Time Decision Support System: A Multi-Layered Framework
authors: Ouarour A.; Hadid M.; Padmanabhan R.; Elomri A.; El Omri A.; Aboumarzouk O.M.; Al-Ansari A.
source_title: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology Management, Operations and Decisions, ICTMOD 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Digital Twins in Surgery as a Real-Time Decision Support System A Multi-Layered Framework.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Surgical care requires real-time coordination among patients, clinicians, devices, and data, often under unpredictable and time-critical conditions. Yet, current hospital systems struggle with adaptability and integration, limiting their ability to respond dynamically during surgery. Digital Twin (DT) technology, initially developed in aerospace and manufacturing, is now emerging as a promising tool for enhancing situational awareness and supporting real-time clinical decisions. This paper presents a multi-layered DT Decision Support System (DT-DSS) architecture tailored to the surgical domain. Drawing from recent literature and scoping reviews, we identify current limitations in model fidelity, system integration, and ethical transparency. We propose a modular framework that spans data ingestion, simulation, predictive optimization, clinical decision-making, and governance. Each architectural layer is backed by validated studies, offering a practical and trustworthy pathway toward real-world surgical implementation. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Real-Time Dynamic Optimization: a Novel Generative Ai Framework for Sub-Second Adaptive Control
authors: Josephthomasrajan A.; Vedavalli S.P.; Varsha E.; Sharmili K.C.; Keerthana S.; Nilavezhil Oviya V.P.
source_title: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference for Advancement in Technology, ICONAT 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Real-Time Dynamic Optimization a Novel Generative Ai Framework for Sub-Second Adaptive Control.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: Inefficiencies in data exchange protocols responsible for 3 2% errors, slow optimization cycles (baseline: 1 2. 7 seconds), and inflexible workflows requiring hours of manual effort pose substantial challenges to modern manufacturing systems. In response to these problems, the study proposes a framework that combines multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) with digital twins, physics-informed systems, and edge-based protocol translation, leveraging AI. The adjustments claimed 26 × faster reconfiguration latency (now 480 ms), 94% precision achieving cross-protocol data precision through 17 standards with containerized converters, and real-time adaptation to workflows. The integration of global evolutionary strategies with local PPO agents demonstrated 89% faster policy convergence than conventional deep Q-networks, 93% simulation-physical correlation via physics-constrained autoencoders, and 1 9. 2% energy reduction against ISO 50001 benchmarks. NIST AI RMF compliance is provided by blockchain-immutable audit trails while distributed MARL training in Ray/PettingZoo environments decreased manual intervention by 92%. Empirical validation reveals the potential to reduce annual downtime by 4 7 billion. Future extensions using generative AI aim to shift global manufacturing CO2 emissions by 18-22%, transforming the vision of autonomous, sustainable production systems. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Understanding digital twins as a digital counterpart of physical systems
authors: Mishra B.K.; Thakker D.; Pervez Z.
source_title: The Digital Twin Handbook: Challenges, opportunities and future research directions
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Understanding digital twins as a digital counterpart of physical systems.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Digital twins (DTs) are virtual copies of physical objects, processes or systems that enable real-time monitoring, analysis, optimisation and modelling. DTs can enhance resource utilisation, improve productivity and contribute across diverse industries and domains. Defining a DT model involves addressing three key challenges: digital transformation, cultural transformation and bridging the skills gap. Digital transformation replicates phy sical systems digitally, while cultural transformation fosters trust in data-driven approa ches. Bridging the skills gap equips organisations to effectively develop and utilise DTS. Despite their transformative potential, implementing DTs involves challenges and con siderations that require careful navigation, as these abstract representations cannot fully replicate all physical system intricacies and overconfidence in their accuracy may lead to overlooked flaws during real-world applications. Privacy concerns emerge with data col lection for DT deployment, risks of surveillance and system privacy. Similarly, DTs may also face challenges suchashighcosts, ethicalconcernsandtheneedforaccurate, real-time data integration. In addition, DTs’ can be vulnerable to risks like cyberattacks and data breaches due to reliance on interconnected digital systems. However, a systematic DT implementation process that includes setting objectives, collecting reliable data, develop ing virtual models, iterative testing, deploying validated models and monitoring perfor mance can minimise these risks. Besides, user training can ensure knowledge gaps and facilitate improvement. As a futuristic DTs scope, it requires collaboration among acade mia, governments and industries to establish standardised approaches addressing inter operability, privacy and technological barriers for widespread DT adoption. By overcoming these challenges, DTs have the potential to redefine operational strategies, foster innovation and deliver sustainable, smart solutions across various domains. © The Institution of Engineering and Technology and its licensors 2026.

title: AI-Driven Digital Twin Framework for Security Threat Simulation and Compliance Optimization
authors: Venkatesan V.; Arunachalam C.
source_title: 2025 ICCST - International Carnahan Conference on Security Technology, ICCST 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - AI-Driven Digital Twin Framework for Security Threat Simulation and Compliance Optimization.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: Security operations face increasing complexity due to evolving cyber threats and regulatory compliance requirements. Traditional incident response methods often suffer from delayed triage and limited readiness for compliance audits. This paper presents an AI-driven Digital Twin framework designed to simulate and analyze security threats, enabling faster incident triage and improved compliance readiness. The proposed system leverages serverless data engineering pipelines, real-time streaming analytics, and AI/ML-driven anomaly detection models integrated within a sandboxed Digital Twin environment. By orchestrating telemetry ingestion (e.g., logs, IoT sensor data, and network events) with scalable cloud native services, the framework supports high-frequency threat simulations without impacting production systems. Experimental evaluations using synthetic and public cybersecurity datasets (CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15) demonstrate up to 40% faster compliance reporting, 35% faster incident triage, and improved anomaly detection accuracy compared to both SIEM and SOAR workflows. Unlike traditional cyber ranges or static compliance tools, this work integrates AI-driven anomaly detection with Digital Twin modeling and serverless pipelines, enabling elastic, real-time what-if threat simulations and automated compliance posture reporting validated against SIEM and SOAR baselines.  © 2025 IEEE.

title: Historic building information modelling: history research hotspots and emerging trends
authors: Xu L.; Zhang Q.; Li Y.; Li Y.; Ren J.; Kong S.; Ding D.; Sang K.; Lin G.; Giordano A.
source_title: Journal of Asian Architecture and Building Engineering
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access; Green Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Historic building information modelling history research hotspots and emerging trends.pdf
level_1_notes: Title is clearly outside production/manufacturing and lacks transferable validation/update signal.
abstract: The rapid adoption of Historic Building Information Modelling (HBIM) in cultural heritage preservation necessitates a comprehensive understanding of its global research trajectories, technological focus areas, and collaborative dynamics. This study conducts a systematic bibliometric analysis of 491 publications from the Web of Science Core Collection (2013–2025), mapping the evolution of HBIM-related research. The results reveal a triphasic growth pattern: initial emergence (2013–2016), consolidation (2017–2019), and exponential expansion (2020–2024), with cumulative publications increasing 117-fold. Geospatial analysis highlights significant regional disparities: European countries lead in productivity, citation impact, and collaborative networks, driven by the density of UNESCO heritage sites and supportive policy frameworks. In contrast, China demonstrates a productivity-impact paradox despite rapid output growth. Keyword co-occurrence and clustering analyses identify key thematic areas, including 3D modeling, interoperability challenges, conservation management, and multidisciplinary methodologies. While emerging areas of technological interest, such as domain-specific AI, semantic segmentation, immersive media, and digital twins, are identified, these findings reflect trends in the published literature rather than in-depth evaluations of practical implementation. The study provides an overview of HBIM’s disciplinary development and offers a foundation for future research, particularly in exploring the real-world application and integration of advanced technologies within cultural heritage preservation. © 2025 The Author(s). Published by Informa UK Limited, trading as Taylor & Francis Group on behalf of the Architectural Institute of Japan, Architectural Institute of Korea and Architectural Society of China.

title: Human-in-the-Loop Artificial Intelligence for Asset Management: A Systematic Review of Applications, Methods, and Research Challenges
authors: Industrial N.A.; Singgih M.L.; Gunarta I.K.
source_title: Proceedings of 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data and Software Engineering, ICoDSE 2025
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Human-in-the-Loop Artificial Intelligence for Asset Management A Systematic Review of Applications, Methods, and Research Challenges.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: The novelty of this research lies in the development of a conceptual framework, known as the Human In The Loop (HITL)-AssetOps Framework, which explicitly integrates human roles into the Artificial Intelligence (AI) based asset management cycle to enhance transparency and accountability in decision-making. This study focuses on the application of HITL-AI in vital sectors, including water and infrastructure, energy systems, transportation and logistics, and structures and the built environment. The proposed framework integrates human involvement into the AI-based asset management cycle, from data acquisition to strategic decision-making. This approach allows humans to act as validators, decision overrides, or interactive assistants in supporting AI models. Thus, the system not only produces accurate predictions but also provides accountable recommendations. The study employed a Systematic Literature Review approach, following the PRISMA protocol of the Scopus database. Of the 97 identified articles, 30 were selected for further analysis based on their applications, methods or algorithms, evaluation metrics, and future research challenges. The analysis results show that techniques such as digital twins, federated learning, deep learning, and genetic algorithms are more effective when integrated with human involvement. © 2025 IEEE.

title: Emerging trends of knowledge management in organizations
authors: Nephiphidi M.
source_title: Proceedings of the International Astronautical Congress, IAC
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - Emerging trends of knowledge management in organizations.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: Knowledge and intellectual resources are what determine how well an organization performs. The survival and competitiveness of a company are significantly influenced by knowledge management. Organizational challenges, cultural barriers, technological constraints, human barriers, and environmental barriers are some of the variables that hinder the successful application of knowledge management techniques in organizations and lower organizational performance. In organizations, knowledge is utilized for sustainability and improvement, and it is seen as the product. This paper looks at the newly created general directions of knowledge management in an organization. The goal is to educate and investigate the present and potential future paths of knowledge management. The purpose of knowledge management is to manage an organization's knowledge. To put it simply, these are solutions based on information technology made to facilitate and improve the techniques of information development, storage, transfer, and application. To facilitate knowledge management, knowledge management systems make use of virtual teams, practice communities, or network-based organizations. Knowledge management is aided by technology adaptation such as artificial intelligence, Automated collection of data, and enhanced search features that make it simpler for staff members to locate the data they require within the organization and create user-friendly and accessible information management systems. Making data available, useful knowledge management services, employer involvement, methods for feedback, meetings and discussions, exchange of information, collaborative management, prompt problem-solving, and employees sharing information. With the advancement of technology, knowledge management trends in an organization are integrated or incorporated into digital twin models, which simulate information and save the results in digital copies in audio and video formats. It improves collaboration and knowledge sharing and boosts adaptability. Technologies that deal primarily with explicit knowledge, including search and categorization, are shown to contribute the most to existing solutions. This paper introduces the theme in this issue that deals with technology by doing a survey of some of the technologies used for knowledge management. The outcomes indicate that the organization has primarily employed a mix of positivist, scientific, and multiple approaches. It is recommended that organizations increase employee awareness regarding knowledge sharing, assess the organization's successful knowledge management procedures to ensure they are adaptable, encourage a culture of knowledge sharing, and update inefficient knowledge management technologies. Copyright ©2025 by the International Astronautical Federation (IAF). All rights reserved.

title: From Tacit to Explicit: Using Live Documentation and Feedback Loops to Facilitate Knowledge Transfer
authors: Stürzebecher P.; Bayrhammer E.; Brauckmann N.
source_title: Proceedings of the European Conference on Knowledge Management, ECKM
open_access_note: All Open Access; Gold Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2025 - From Tacit to Explicit Using Live Documentation and Feedback Loops to Facilitate Knowledge Transfer.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks DT-like model or simulation/model signal.
abstract: The preservation and structured use of tacit knowledge (TK) is a critical challenge in industrial environments contending with increasing automation and a skilled labor shortage. The loss of undocumented expertise, especially in circular economy applications such as disassembly processes, threatens process efficiency, adaptability and quality. This paper presents a knowledge management approach that combines industrial engineering methods with Industry 4.0 technologies to capture and integrate TK into semiautomated disassembly systems digitally. Taking Fraunhofer IFF’s iDeaR project as a case study, a demonstrator is developed to document and convert experts’ actions during PC disassembly into machine-readable formats. The approach integrates live documentation, feedback loops and digital twins to systematically capture contextual problem-solving strategies, enabling their reuse and continuous learning in technical systems. Tacit knowledge is structured using a dedicated Asset Administration Shell (AAS) submodel, comprising situational context, problem description, solution, guidance and benefit. This facilitates contextual reuse across diverse scenarios. The demonstrator architecture links captured knowledge with product, process and resource twins and provides context-sensitive support through modular software applications. Expert-reviewed feedback loops transform raw data into validated disassembly instructions, checklists and training content. A user-friendly interface facilitates intuitive data entry and practical applicability in industrial settings. Results from a workshop-based analysis of disassembly steps confirm that both implicit and explicit knowledge can be meaningfully structured and evaluated for automation capability. The approach preserves expertise, enhances organizational learning and contributes to more adaptive, error-resistant processes. Future developments include AI-assisted storytelling and enhanced sensor integration to further improve feedback quality and reduce editing. This paper thus contributes to the design of intelligent knowledge systems for (semi)automated environments and highlights the value of digital knowledge models in industrial transformation. © 2025 Academic Conferences Limited. All rights reserved.

title: Model-Driven Development Towards Distributed Intelligent Systems
authors: Barriga A.; Barriga J.A.; Pérez-Toledano M.A.; Clemente P.J.
open_access_note: All Open Access; Bronze Open Access
suggested_pdf_filename: 2024 - Model-Driven Development Towards Distributed Intelligent Systems.pdf
level_1_notes: Title lacks validation/update and production/manufacturing signals.
abstract: A Distributed Intelligent System (DIS) encompasses a set of intelligent subsystems and components that collaborate to perform tasks and solve problems. Given the advancements of paradigms such as the Internet of Things, along with the advancements of technologies such as Machine Learning and Digital Twins, DISs are on the rise. These systems are increasingly integrating components that perform intelligent functions, and these intelligent functions are increasingly heterogeneous and varied. Moreover, there is no standardized framework to help researchers and practitioners adequately address DISs. As a result, the complexity, interoperability issues, and development time and costs of these systems are growing. However, Model-Driven Development (MDD) can help to address these challenges by providing a Domain-Specific Language (DSL) for developing DISs. In this work, a DSL for the design, validation, generation, and deployment of DISs is proposed. Firstly, the proposed DSL captures in a metamodel the key and high-level abstract concepts of the distinct DISs documented in the literature. Then, it allows modeling of DISs conforming to this metamodel. Subsequently, the DSL enables formal validation of the modeled systems. Lastly, it allows the generation and deployment of all DISs into production. Therefore, the work undertaken in this communication provides a methodological, formal, and standardized approach to defining and developing DISs from a high level of abstraction. This work allows users to address DISs by facilitating agility, minimizing manual tasks, and reducing the number of defects introduced in their development.
